data.title: As a new project member, I am asked about Jira, Figma, and Confluence integrations during onboarding so I don't have to discover and configure them later
data.description:
## Description

**What**: Add Figma and Confluence as optional integration steps alongside existing Jira during Phase 2 Socratic dialogue, with credential safety guardrails
**Why**: Figma and Confluence integrations are silently skipped, forcing users to discover and configure them manually later
**Scope**:
- IN: Add Figma and Confluence questions to Phase 2 dialogue, each labeled as skippable with one-sentence explanation, credentials (PATs) NEVER in dso-config.conf, non-sensitive identifiers only in config, explicit disabled sentinel written on skip
- OUT: Implementing the actual Figma/Confluence integration functionality, validating credentials at question time

## Done Definitions

- When this story is complete, onboarding asks about Jira, Figma, and Confluence during Phase 2, each clearly labeled as skippable with a one-sentence explanation
  ← Satisfies: "onboarding asks about Jira, Figma, and Confluence integrations as optional configuration steps"
- When this story is complete, credentials (API tokens, PATs) are never written to dso-config.conf — only non-sensitive identifiers are stored, with credentials referenced as environment variables
  ← Satisfies: "Credentials are NEVER written to dso-config.conf"
- When this story is complete, skipping an integration writes an explicit disabled sentinel to config
  ← Satisfies: scenario analysis concern about ambiguous config state
- Unit tests written and passing for all new or modified logic

## Considerations

- [Security] Figma PAT must follow existing Jira env-var credential pattern — never in config
- [Testing] Skip path should write explicit disabled sentinel to disambiguate skipped from never-configured

## Escalation Policy

**Escalation policy**: Proceed unless a significant assumption is required to continue — one that could send the implementation in the wrong direction. Escalate only when genuinely blocked without a reasonable inference. Document all assumptions made without escalating.
